channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, top pollster for 'brazen election interference,' fraud over Harris poll
item.description: FIRST ON FOX: President-elect Donald Trump is suing the Des Moines Register and its top pollster Ann Selzer for “brazen election interference" and fraud over its final 2024 presidential poll showing Vice President Kamala Harris leading him in Iowa, despite his ultimate victory in the state by m
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction because of Supreme Court immunity ruling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top Harris aide hypes radical activist who said 'America deserved 9/11' while plotting future for Dems
item.description: A top aide on Harris' failed presidential campaign recently called for more cultural voices like Hasan Piker, who previously faced backlash for saying "America deserved 9/11.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rejects Trump request to toss Bragg charges in New York on basis of presidential immunity
item.description: Judge Juan Merchan on Monday rejected Trump attorneys' request to dismiss charges brought against him by Manhattan District Attorney Alvin Bragg on the basis of presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that made President-elect Trump a felon, ruling the conviction in the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schumer seeks legislation giving local officials authority to ‘swiftly’ respond to drone sightings
item.description: Sen. Chuck Schumer, a Democrat from New York, is seeking to have legislation passed that provides local officials greater authority to respond to possible drone sightings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says it was 'stupid' for Biden to let Ukraine use US weapons to strike deeper into Russia
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Monday suggested that he may reverse President Joe Biden's recent decision to allow Ukrainian forces to use American long-range weapons to strike deeper into Russian territory. Trump called the decision made by Biden last month “stupid.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's a 'believer' in polio vaccine, and other news conference takeaways
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Monday held a wide-ranging news conference in which he said he would preserve access to the polio vaccine but equivocated on other vaccines, pledged to look at bringing down the costs of pharmaceuticals and expressed doubts that his daughter-in-law might be Florida's
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: The DNC's Blue Christmas
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, featuring the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What's behind rising autism rates: A broader definition of autism and better screening
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump seemed to entertain the discredited theory that vaccines cause autism as he answered questions from journalists Monday at his Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers signal movement toward government funding deal
item.description: Top Republicans are signaling progress in government funding talks as leaders look to clinch a deal ahead of a looming Friday deadline. House Appropriations Chair Tom Cole (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden admin expected to offer California waiver to ban gas-powered cars by 2035: report
item.description: The Biden administration is reportedly considering giving California permission to ban new gas-powered vehicles in the state within a decade before the Democrat's term comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams' former chief advisor expects to be indicted: lawyer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Donald Trump says he would consider pardoning NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- President-elect Donald Trump on Monday said he would consider pardoning Mayor Eric Adams after his historic indictment on federal charges. "I think that he was treated pretty unfairly," Trump said at a press conference in Florida. "I would certainly look at it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Paris Hilton urges House to pass 'Stop Institutional Child Abuse Act' after Senate's unanimous approval
item.description: Paris Hilton, emotional after the Senate unanimously passed the Stop Institutional Child Abuse Act, urges the GOP-led House to approve the bill she has championed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capitol Hill braces for high-stakes showdown over $36T US debt crisis
item.description: A new economic projection suggests Congress could be headed for a showdown over the U.S. debt limit by mid-June.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump weighs in on NY mayor, vaccines and drones in freewheeling press conference at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump told reporters that he would consider pardoning embattled New York Mayor Eric Adams, said the country was “not going to lose” the polio vaccine and weighed in on the flurry of drone sightings over New Jersey during a freewheeling press conference Monday at his Mar-a-Lago
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Jill Colvin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court won’t block order directing Peter Navarro to turn over emails
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to weigh Peter Navarro’s bid to reverse a court order directing him to turn over hundreds of emails sent during his tenure as an adviser in the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Paris Hilton heading to Capitol with 'urgent' plea to pass youth welfare program reform
item.description: Paris Hilton is heading back to the Capitol with an urgent plea, calling on "every member of the House" to pass a bill focused on youth congregate care facilities that she says would "create real, meaningful change" before the legislative session ends this week.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Former FBI informant will plead guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: A former FBI informant is set to plead guilty to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son, Hunter, that became central to a Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Speaker Johnson faces 'complicated' decision on crucial Rules Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is barreling into a tricky — yet critical — decision over whom to appoint as the next chair of the House Rules Committee, a post that will set the tone for legislating and play a key role in keeping Republicans united in next year’s ultrathin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: When Donald Trump takes office as president for a second time, many journalists anticipate a hostile climate toward them and their work.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Former longtime South Carolina congressman John Spratt dies at 82
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — John Spratt, a former longtime Democratic congressman from South Carolina who successfully pushed for a balanced budget deal in the 1990s but was unseated decades later when his district turned Republican, has died. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey drones: State senator wants state of emergency
item.description: One politician in New Jersey is calling for a "limited" state of emergency after what he said is a lack of government transparency over the recent unexplained wave of drone sightings.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers tell Apple, Google to prepare for TikTok ban
item.description: TikTok now plans to appeal to the Supreme Court and has asked the D.C. Circuit to put the law on hold while it appeals the case.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Momentum builds around Social Security reforms ahead of Senate vote
item.description: Momentum is building around legislation that could mean higher Social Security benefits for some Americans as Senate leadership tees up a vote for next week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz reflects on Democrats’ shortcomings, return to Minnesota as political ground shifts
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz is back at the Capitol and reacclimating to life after his vice presidential bid. Politics Friday host Brian Bakst and senior politics reporter Dana Ferguson unpack an interview with Walz about three months on the national stage and what awaits next year.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, Dana Ferguson, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Judge rejects an attempt by Trump campaign lawyer to invalidate guilty plea in Georgia election case
item.description: The judge overseeing the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others on Friday rejected an attempt by former Trump campaign lawyer Kenneth Chesebro to invalidate his guilty plea.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Hill to chair House Financial Services Committee, Walberg to lead Education and Workforce panel
item.description: House Republican leaders on Thursday choose Rep. French Hill (R-Ark.) to lead the House Financial Services Committee in the 119th Congress and Rep. Tim Walberg (R-Mich.) to chair the House Committee on Education and the Workforce, completing their selections for committee chairs.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Border Report Live: 'Border czar' accepts Texas land as others push back on deportations
item.description: Tom Homan told Dr. Phil he's already working on a plan for borderlands the Texas offered the Trump administration's "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota cities take different approaches to recreational cannabis market
item.description: The Minnesota Legislature set most of the rules about cultivation and sale of recreational marijuana in spring 2023. However, the state left several key decisions within local control, including whether cities can allow more than a set minimum of shops.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr.'s daughter-in-law met with Ratcliffe about CIA job: Washington Post
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s daughter-in-law took a private meeting with John Ratcliffe, President-elect Trump’s pick to run the CIA, in an apparent bid for a high-ranking position within the agency, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, Fox 5
